title: RA.981 MALUGI
tracklist:

translation.content: Club Heart Broken is perhaps a misnomer. The party and label, founded in Cologne by MALUGI, may take its name from one of life's tougher emotions, but the vibe is all about unfettered, unadulterated joy. It's a state of mind its founder fully embodies—he's called himself the "happiest man in dance music." (We tried fact-checking this, but watching him perform seems proof enough.) Club Heart Broken's motto is simple: a party should be fun. A "party for lovers, loners and losers," the crew also made up of ferrari rot, SURF 2 GLORY and fellow maximalist Marlon Hoffstadt represent the sound of young Berlin in the 2020s, as notorious as they are beloved. Since relocating from Cologne, the event has snapped the German's capital infamously serious techno stereotype with its unpretentious, anything-goes music policy. It's made MALUGI and Hoffstadt especially in-demand worldwide—queues used to extend out of the door of Watergate and across Oberbaumbrücke Musically, MALUGI is the most eclectic in the crew. His metaphorical record bag carries a lot of label material, from zingy Eurodance to upbeat pumpers, but also plenty of steppy, tuff UK bass music. Releases from Main Phase and Interplanetary Criminal, the likes of dubplate label ec2a, and even the odd Big Ang record pepper his sets, much of which you'll hear here. MALUGI's RA Podcast is all big feelings for big rooms. As he details in the interview below, it's a window into the more house-y side of his sound. From bubbling garage to chunky chords, RA.981 is a testament to MALUGI's belief: the best parties should leave you grinning, not crying. – Bella Aquilina What have you been up to recently? After returning from my Australian tour in mid-January, I've taken a few weeks off from touring, which has given me the time to write a lot of new music. I've been locking myself in the studio with some incredible artists to work on fresh tunes that I can't wait to share with my audience very soon. I've also had a lot of time to dig for new pearls for my sets, so my USB is fresher than ever and I'm more than ready to play all of these new bangers out in the coming weeks and months. How and where was the mix recorded? And can you tell us the idea behind it? I recorded the mix on my home setup in Berlin. As I've found all of this great new music lately, I had all the options where to go with the mix and felt very inspired to explore the more house-y side of my sound. So I'm going on a journey through some of my favourite genres, from UKG to piano bangers, from tech house to more proggy sounds. Of course I couldn't finish a mix without showcasing some of my incredibly talented friends' music and some of my own unreleased tracks. What are you looking forward to in the near future? I'm definitely looking forward to a festival season full of highlights, but before that I'm very honoured to be making my debut at Panorama Bar at the end of March for the Live from Earth showcase alongside the most wonderful lineup possible. What's one social or political cause you want the world to pay more attention to? I would like to take the opportunity to draw attention to civil sea rescue and Sea Watch in particular. There have been times where this cause was very visible, but there's not enough attention recently for how important it is. Especially at a time when right-wing parties are gaining ground in Europe and politicians are only talking about defending borders, it is more important than ever to support organisations that stand up for humanity and try to ensure that no refugee has to die in Europe's seas while seeking help. What's one club or party that had a major impact on you as an artist? The Amsterdam club music scene around FS Green, Jarreau Vandal and Jael was very influential on me. Jael's talent as a musician, Jarreau Vandal's stage presence and FS Green's approach to dance music are still unmatched for me. When I first discovered their music I went to club nights like Einhundert in Berlin, Les Amis in Hamburg or 99 Ginger in Paris and the vibe at those parties was like nothing I'd ever experienced before. The crowds were dancing ecstatically and singing along to the tunes. I experienced this as very liberating, and it ultimately inspired me to start the MALUGI project.
translation.blurb: Big feelings for big rooms with the ringleader of Club Heart Broken.
